pi_items.source: setup_task.sh, lines 17-77: XML file is generated at this path containing patient John Doe with Penicillin allergy
export_result.sh, line 14: TEST_PATIENT="VerifyBot User"; used as expected value in verification
export_result.sh, line 15: TEST_SUBSTANCE="Kryptonite"; used as expected value in verification
export_result.sh, line 16: TEST_REACTION="Weakness"; used as expected value in verification

privileged_info_summary: The task requires building a NextGen Connect integration channel to extract patient allergies from C-CDA (Consolidated Clinical Document Architecture) XML files. Input files are read from /home/ga/ccda_input and parsed JSON output is written to /home/ga/allergy_output. The sample C-CDA file at /home/ga/Documents/sample_ccda.xml contains patient John Doe with a Penicillin allergy and Hives reaction. Dynamic verification uses a synthetic test XML with patient name 'VerifyBot User', substance 'Kryptonite', and reaction 'Weakness'. All data is synthetic and generated by setup and verification scripts.
